text: Trump says he is 'not afraid' of boots on the ground in Iran when asked if it could become another Vietnam. First direct signal he is open to ground invasion. Pentagon reportedly preparing options for Hormuz ground operation.
verificationNote: Iran International and New Republic confirmed Trump remarks with Irish PM

text: Tanker hit by projectile near UAE — first attack on Hormuz shipping in 5 days. At least 17 vessels struck since war began. Brent crude settled at $103.42/barrel, up 3.2% and now 42% higher since conflict started.
verificationNote: Alma Research Center daily report and oil market data confirmed

text: Senate Democrats led by Booker, Murphy, Kaine threatening to force repeated war powers votes and slow Senate business this week. Filed 5 resolutions demanding troop withdrawal. Republicans still blocking public hearings on the war.
verificationNote: PBS, The Hill, and Fortune confirmed Democratic strategy and filed resolutions

text: AI-generated war misinformation reaching unprecedented levels — state actors flooding social media with fake videos of explosions, destroyed cities, and downed carriers. ABC News identifies both Iranian and Russian influence operations behind coordinated disinfo campaigns.
verificationNote: ABC News, Rolling Stone, and Poynter fact-check reporting confirmed state-backed disinfo campaigns

text: Israel kills Iran intelligence minister Esmail Khatib in overnight Tehran airstrike — third senior official eliminated in 48 hours. Defense Minister Katz announces all Iranian officials now authorized targets.
Iran fires cluster munitions at central Israel for first time — Khorramshahr-4 and Qadr missiles hit Ramat Gan killing elderly couple. IRGC explicitly frames as revenge for Larijani assassination.

South Pars gas field struck by Israeli jets — two refineries halted (100M cubic meters/day). First upstream energy strike of the war. Brent spikes. Iran threatens retaliation against Saudi, UAE, Qatar energy sites.
Turkey FM Fidan heading to Riyadh for multilateral meeting. 10+ countries attending including Pakistan, Egypt, UAE, Qatar. Aim: diplomatic pathway to end Iran war.

Chaharshanbe Suri aftermath: footage shows bonfires across Tehran despite regime crackdown threats. PMOI claims 15 resistance operations. Reza Pahlavi says ready to return. Internet still blacked out.
Mojtaba Khamenei rejects ceasefire proposals from two intermediary countries. Says 'not the right time for peace.' Still no public appearance since appointment — health status unknown.
US CENTCOM deploys 5,000-lb bunker busters against hardened Iranian antiship missile sites along Hormuz. Analysts call it opening salvo of multi-week campaign to reopen strait.

Iran strikes Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility — world's largest. QatarEnergy reports 'extensive damage.' Qatar immediately expels Iran's military attaches. War now directly hitting allied Gulf energy infrastructure.
Gabbard omitted from testimony that Iran was NOT rebuilding enrichment before war. Bloomberg caught discrepancy between written and spoken testimony. Major blow to war justification narrative.

USS Ford leaving Red Sea for Crete repairs after fire. Only Lincoln remains. Iran simultaneously hitting UAE (13 missiles, 27 drones), Saudi (4 missiles, 21 drones), and Qatar (Ras Laffan). Conflict fully regional.
8 non-Iranian vessels detected transiting Hormuz — nearly double recent days. Iran allowing 'permission-based transits to friendly countries.' Traffic still down ~97% overall.

IAEA reveals new underground enrichment site at Isfahan never inspected. Iran stockpile could fuel 9 nuclear weapons. Grossi: war has caused 'enormous degradation' but enrichment capabilities will likely survive.
Senate defeats Booker war powers resolution 53-47 for third time. Democrats pledge weekly votes. Gabbard told Senate Iran regime 'intact but largely degraded.'
Trump issues 60-day Jones Act waiver for foreign ships. Treasury eases Venezuela PDVSA sanctions. Analysts say combined gas price impact ~3 cents/gal.
UANI identifies 25 Iranian tankers in Gulf carrying ~20M barrels ($1B+ revenue for IRGC). 7 dark fleet tankers heading to Malaysia for China-bound transfers.
Fed holds rates at 3.5-3.75%. Dow crashes 768 points. Hot PPI at 0.7%. Only 1 cut projected for 2026. Markets fear stagflation.
Riyadh emergency meeting of 10+ Arab/Islamic FMs convenes without US. Missile alerts sound in Saudi capital during session. Germany's Merz openly criticizes war.
Speaker Johnson says 'mission just about accomplished' but Hormuz 'dragging it out.' WFP warns 45M more could face acute hunger if war continues through June.
